title: The Craft Of Frankenstein: Production Design, Cinematography, Editing, Costume & Score
text: Deadline's deep dive into the craftsmanship behind Guillermo del Toro's Frankenstein explores how the production design, cinematography, editing, costume design, and musical score came together to create the film's distinctive aesthetic. The production design by Tamara Deverell creates an operatic, hand-crafted world with a carefully coded color palette centered on red. The design work integrates symbolic circular and cylinder motifs throughout the spaces, from the water tower laboratory to the Arctic exploration sets. The cinematography captures these elaborately designed spaces, with almost all illumination originating from outside the sets, shooting through massive operatic windows. The costume design by Kate Hawley reflects del Toro's character bibles and visual references, drawing influences from David Bowie and Prince. The film represents a comprehensive artistic vision where production design serves as the foundation for all other crafts to build upon.
